properties.remark: WATER ROSE RAPIDLY NEAR MANVEL DUE TO A DOWNSTREAM ICE JAM AND BACKWATER ON THE TURTLE RIVER. WATER ROSE OVER 3 FEET AND THREATENED HOMES NEAREST THE RIVER...WITH SOME HOMES REQUIRING EMERGENCY SANDBAG PROTECTION. BREAKOUT FLOWS FROM THE RIVER ALSO FLOODED THE GOLF COURSE AND OVERTOPPED HWY 33 TO THE WEST OF TOWN.

THE SHEYENNE RIVER ROSE RAPIDLY THROUGH CENTRAL PORTIONS OF LISBON...IN RESPOSE TO AN ICE JAM WHICH FORMED NEAR THE HWY 27 BRIDGE. FLOOD WATERS ROSE ABOVE THE MODERATE FLOOD STAGE BY 11 PM CDT AND EVENTUALLY CRESTED ABOVE THE MAJOR FLOOD STAGE. EMERGENCY LEVEES HAD BEEN CONSTRUCTED IN ADVANCE AND EMERGENCY CREWS WERE ABLE TO CONTAIN THE FLOODING.

*** 1 FATAL, 5 INJ *** THIS TORNADO CROSSED FROM DOUGLAS COUNTY NORTHWARD INTO OTTER TAIL COUNTY AND PRODUCED AN ADDITIONAL 38 MILES OF CONTINUOUS DAMAGE PATH... ENDING ABOUT 10 MILES NORTH OF BLUFFTON BY 445 PM CDT. THE TORNADO DESTROYED NUMEROUS HOMES AND FARMSTEADS ALONG ITS PATH... COMPLETELY SWEEPING 7 HOMES FROM THEIR FOUNDATIONS. AT TIMES... THIS MULTI-VORTEX TORNADO COMPLEX COSISTED OF MULTIPLE TORNADIC TUBES PIVOTING AROUND A COMMON CENTER. MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 1.3 MILES AND PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 175 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD FOR NEARLY 9 MILES TO ABOUT 12 NORTH OF MAYVILLE BY 405 PM CDT AND CROSSED INTO GRAND FORKS COUNTY. IT THEN CONTINUED FOR ANOTHER 8 MILES TO AROUND 10W THOMPSOM BY 418 PM CDT FOR A TOTAL TRACK LENGTH OF NEARLY 17 MILES. TREES IN SHELTER BELTS AND FARMSTEADS WERE SNAPPED...UPROOTED OR SHEARED OFF. ONE WELL CONSTRUCTED HOUSE NEAR HOLMES ND WAS COMPLETELY SWEPT FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 150 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 185 MPH.

TWO PERSISTENT WALL CLOUDS TRACKED WITH PERIODIC FUNNELS TRACKED FROM NORTH CENTRAL OTTER TAIL COUNTY INTO SOUTH CENTRAL BECKER COUNTY. ONE PRODUCED A BRIEF TORNADIC TOUCHDOWN NEAR ROSE LAKE IN HOBART TOWNSHIP. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 15 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 75 MPH.
GOLF BALL TO HEN EGG SIZED HAIL OCCURRED.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEASTWARD FOR ROUGHLY 4 MILES AND CROSSED INTO POLK COUNTY ABOUT 5NE OF BEJOU AROUND 407 PM CDT. THE TORNADO CONTINUED FOR ANOTHER 2 MILES INTO POLK COUNTY TO AROUND 7 MILES SOUTHWEST OF FOSSTON BY 410 PM CDT. SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN IN FARM SHELTER BELTS. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH WAS ABOUT 6 MILES. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 40 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 80 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD FOR ABOUT A MILE TO AROUND 1 WSW OF BALDHILL DAM BY 403 PM CDT. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A WOODEN POWER POLE... KNOCKED DOWN SEVERAL POPLAR TREES AND SHREADED A PORTION OF AN OLD CORN FIELD. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 100 MPH.

MULTIPLE FUNNEL CLOUDS WERE VIEWED TO THE WEST... SOUTHWEST AND SOUTH OF DAZEY... ALL MOVING NORTHWARD. ONE PRODUCED A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD NEAR DAZEY THOUGH NO DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 15 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 80 MPH.

THIS TORNADO CONTINUED FROM TRAILL COUNTY INTO GRAND FORKS COUNTY AND TRACKED FOR AN ADDITIONAL 8 MILES TO AROUND 10W OF THOMPSOM BY 418 PM CDT. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH WAS ROUGHLY 17 MILES. ONE WELL CONSTRUCTED HOUSE NEAR HOLMES WAS COMPLETELY SWEPT FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED ALONG WITH SEVERAL OTHER FARM BUILDINGS. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO150 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 185 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD OVER OPENED RANGELAND FOR ABOUT A MILE AND CROSSED THE BENSON COUNTY LINE TO NEAR 8W WARWICK BY 428 PM CDT. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 15 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 70 MPH.
TORNADO CROSSED FROM EDDY COUNTY INTO BENSON COUNTY BEFORE DISSIPATING MAINLY OVER OPEN PASTURELAND. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 15 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 70 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD FOR NEARLY 7 MILES TO AROUND 1N BLABON BY 438 PM CDT. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SEVERE TREE DAMAGE IN FIELD AND FARM SHELTER BELTS LOCATED ALONG ITS PATH. REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT WINDS ALSO PRODUCED TREE DAMAGE AT THE HOPE GOLF COURSE. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 75 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 120 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD INTERMITTENTLY FOR NEARLY 7 MILES TO AROUND 3NE HOPE BY 439 PM CDT. TREES WERE DAMAGED IN SHELTER BELTS AND FARM YARDS. A CENTER PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS TUMBLED AND TWISTED NEAR COLGATE. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 50 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD INTERMITTENTLY FOR ABOUT 7 MILES TO NEAR 1E MECKINOCK BY 442 PM CDT. A POLE BARN AND SEVERAL FARM OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. DEBRIS WAS THROWN NEAR EMERADO AND JUST EAST OF THE GRAND FORKS AIR FORCE BASE. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 100 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 120 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTH NORTHEASTWARD FOR ROUGHLY A MILE ACROSS PORTIONS OF LONG LOST LAKE TO ABOUT 8S OF ZERKEL BY 434 PM CDT. NUMEROUS LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN AND LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 40 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 90 MPH.
DEBRIS DAMAGE AT EMERADO TRAILER PARK. FUNNEL LIFTED OFF THE GROUND AS IT CROSSED NORTHWARD OVER HIGHWAY 2.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEASTWARD FOR NEARLY 3 MILES TO AROUND 8ENE OF HOPE BY 445 PM CDT. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED IN SHELTER BELTS. AN ABANDONED FARMHOUSE WAS COLLAPSED AND OTHER FARM BUILDING DAMAGED. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTMATED TO 75 YARDS WITH PEAK WINDS TO 125 MPH.
TREE DAMAGE AT THE HOPE GOLF COURSE.

TORNADO TRACKED NEARLY DUE NORTH FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO 2NNW OF THE RICE LAKE COMMUNITY BY 446 PM CDT. THE TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN NUMEROUS LARGE TREE LIMBS AND UPROOTED OR SNAPPED OFF SEVERAL TREES. SOME SHINGLES... GUTTERS AND TRIM PIECES WERE TORN OFF OF A HOUSE. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 30 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 95 MPH.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE GRAND FORKS AIRPORT AND TRACKED NORTHWARD FOR NEARLY 10 MILES TO AROUND 1W MANVEL BY 504 PM CDT. THE TORNADO DID LIFT BRIEFLY AROUND 501 PM CDT BEFORE REFORMING WITH TWO TORNADIC VORTICES EXTENDING FROM A COMMON WALL CLOUD. THE TWIN TORNADOES THEN TRACKED CYCLICALLY UP TO A MILE APART WHILE THEY SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES IN SHELTER BELTS AND COLLAPSED A POLE BARN. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 50 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 100 MPH.
LARGE TORNADO WITH DEBRIS MOVING NORTH.

THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN OTTER TAIL COUNTY AND TRACKED NORTHEASTWARD FOR ABOUT 2 MILES BEFORE CROSSING INTO WADENA COUNTY JUST SOUTHWEST OF THE WADENA CITY LIMITS AROUND 502 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED RAPIDLY INTO A MULTI-VORTEX WEDGE TYPE TORNADO AS IT APPROACHED WADENA... AND CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 8 MILES IN WADENA COUNTY UNTIL ABOUT 516 PM CDT. OTTER TAIL COUNTY RECEIVED MAINLY EXTREME TREE DAMAGE. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 1.1 MILES AND PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 170 MPH.

TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 2 MILES TO NEAR 5SSE NORTHWOOD BY 505 PM CDT. THE TORNADO WAS VIEWED BY SPOTTERS NEAR GOLDEN LAKE AND THE TOWN OF HATTON. SEVERAL TREE TOPS WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE GOOSE RIVER. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 50 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 80 MPH.

*** 20 INJ *** THIS MULTI-VORTEX WEDGE TORNADO CONTINUED FROM OTTER TAIL COUNTY NORTHEASTWARD TO ABOUT 7 NORTHEAST OF WADENA BY 516 PM CDT. THE TORNADO RAVAGED THE WESTERN HALF OF THE COMMUNITY OF WADENA. NUMEROUS HOMES WERE FLATTENED TO THE GROUND AND AT LEAST ONE WAS COMPLETELY SWEPT FROM ITS FOUNDATIONS AND DESTROYED. PUBLIC AND INDUSTRIAL BUILDING WERE WRECKED. SCHOOL BUSES AND VEHICLES WERE PROPELLED THROUGH THE AIR FOR HUNDREDS OF YARDS. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 1.1 MILES AND PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 170 MPH.
MULTIPLE VORTEX TORNADO CONFIRMED IN WADENA.

TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWARD INTERMITTENTLY FOR ROUGHLY 2 MILES TO ABOUT 1SE OF GOODRIDGE BY 510 PM CDT. THE TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS AND SNAPPED OFF SOME SMALLER TREES. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 80 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS TO 100 MPH.
